tavily.answer: Uganda has closed its border with the Democratic Republic of Congo due to Ebola fears, with seven confirmed cases in Kampala. The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a "public health emergency of international concern," with over 80 suspected deaths, eight laboratory-confirmed cases, and 246 suspected cases in Ituri province. Uganda has implemented strict health screening and monitoring protocols for exceptions like authorized Ebola response teams and essential transportation. The outbreak is driven by the Bundibugyo strain of the virus.
